summary: Log Analytics workspaces, Application Insights components, and metric alerts. Retention, ingestion caps, and sampling are self-service cost/visibility knobs; silencing an alert stays guardrailed since it is a monitoring-posture change.

operations.title: Update Log Analytics workspace tags
operations.summary: Set or update this workspace's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone.
operations.description: Sets or merges tags on the Log Analytics workspace. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, no impact on ingested data or queries.

params.name: workspace
params.label: Log Analytics workspace
params.help: The workspace to tag.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched. Azure tag names cannot contain < > % & \ ? /.

decisions: Tags are metadata only — cost-neutral and freely reversible — so this stays LOW risk.

operations.title: Set Log Analytics workspace retention
operations.summary: Change how long ingested log data is kept. Shortening the window permanently discards data older than the new limit.
operations.description: Sets retention_in_days on the Log Analytics workspace. Azure supports 30-730 days. Extending retention raises storage cost; shortening it permanently discards data outside the new window on Azure's next retention sweep — there is no undo for data already purged.

params.name: workspace
params.label: Log Analytics workspace
params.help: The workspace to change.

params.name: new_retention_in_days
params.label: Retention (days)
params.help: Days of log data to retain; Azure accepts 30-730. Lowering this permanently discards data older than the new window.

decisions: Cost-changing — longer retention bills more storage — so MEDIUM floor.
Shortening the window is a real data-loss risk once Azure's retention sweep runs — called out explicitly since the op does not warn again at apply time.
Config-only change to the workspace resource; nothing restarts, so downtime stays none.

operations.title: Set the Log Analytics daily ingestion cap
operations.summary: Cap how much log data the workspace ingests per day, or remove the cap. A cap stops ingestion once it is hit.
operations.description: Sets daily_quota_gb on the Log Analytics workspace, capping daily ingestion volume as a cost guardrail. -1 means no cap (unlimited ingestion, the Azure default). Once a day's ingestion hits the cap, Azure stops collecting new data for the rest of that day — including data any alert rule depends on — until the daily reset.

params.name: workspace
params.label: Log Analytics workspace
params.help: The workspace to change.

params.name: new_daily_quota_gb
params.label: Daily ingestion cap (GB)
params.help: Daily ingestion cap in GB; use -1 to remove the cap entirely. Hitting the cap stops log collection for the rest of that day.

decisions: Cost-changing in both directions — raising or removing the cap increases ingestion spend — so MEDIUM floor.
A cap that is hit silently stops log collection (and any alert that depends on that data) for the rest of the day — an operational risk worth flagging, not just a cost one.
Config-only; no restart, so downtime stays none.

operations.title: Update Application Insights tags
operations.summary: Set or update this component's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone.
operations.description: Sets or merges tags on the Application Insights component. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, no impact on collected telemetry.

params.name: app_insights
params.label: Application Insights component
params.help: The Application Insights component to tag.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched. Azure tag names cannot contain < > % & \ ? /.

decisions: Tags are metadata only — cost-neutral and freely reversible — so this stays LOW risk.

operations.title: Set Application Insights retention
operations.summary: Change how long telemetry data is kept. Shortening the window permanently discards data older than the new limit.
operations.description: Sets retention_in_days on the Application Insights component to one of Azure's supported retention windows. Extending retention raises storage cost; shortening it permanently discards telemetry outside the new window.

params.name: app_insights
params.label: Application Insights component
params.help: The Application Insights component to change.

params.name: new_retention_in_days
params.label: Retention (days)
params.help: Application Insights only accepts these discrete retention windows, in days; 90 is the Azure default.

decisions: Cost-changing — longer retention bills more storage — so MEDIUM floor, matching the Log Analytics retention op.
Shortening the window permanently discards telemetry once Azure's retention sweep runs.
Retention only accepts a closed set of values on this resource, so this is an allowlist rather than a min/max range.

operations.title: Set Application Insights sampling rate
operations.summary: Change what share of telemetry is collected. Lower sampling cuts cost but also cuts visibility during an incident.
operations.description: Sets sampling_percentage on the Application Insights component. 100 collects every telemetry item; lower values collect a random share to cut ingestion cost, at the cost of losing some visibility — potentially including the exact request that mattered during an incident.

params.name: app_insights
params.label: Application Insights component
params.help: The Application Insights component to change.

params.name: new_sampling_percentage
params.label: Sampling percentage
params.help: Percent of telemetry to keep, 0-100. 100 keeps everything and is also Azure's default.

decisions: Cost-changing — ingestion volume scales with the sampling rate — so MEDIUM floor.
Lowering sampling trades cost for visibility — flagged since a too-low rate can hide the one request an incident review needs.
Config-only; no restart, so downtime stays none.

operations.title: Enable or silence a metric alert rule
operations.summary: Turn this alert rule on or off. Silencing it stops notifications until it is re-enabled — review before applying.
operations.description: Sets enabled on the metric alert rule. false silences the rule — Azure Monitor stops evaluating it and no notifications fire — without deleting its configuration, so re-enabling later restores it exactly as it was. Both directions are offered: silencing during planned maintenance and re-enabling afterward are equally legitimate self-service asks.

params.name: alert
params.label: Metric alert rule
params.help: The alert rule to enable or silence.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Enabled
params.help: false silences the rule (no evaluation, no notifications) without deleting it; true restores normal evaluation.

decisions: Silencing an alert is a real monitoring-posture change — an incident could go unnoticed while it is off — so MEDIUM floor even though the change itself is trivially reversible.
Both true and false are offered since planned-maintenance silencing and re-enabling afterward are both legitimate, equally common asks.
Config-only; no restart, so downtime stays none.

operations.title: Update metric alert tags
operations.summary: Set or update this alert rule's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone.
operations.description: Sets or merges tags on the metric alert rule. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, no impact on alert evaluation.

params.name: alert

params.help: The alert rule to tag.

params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched. Azure tag names cannot contain < > % & \ ? /.

decisions: Tags are metadata only — cost-neutral and freely reversible — so this stays LOW risk.

operations.title: Create a Log Analytics workspace
operations.summary: Request a new Log Analytics workspace to collect logs and metrics; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Specify a new Log Analytics workspace: name, resource group, region, pricing tier, and retention window. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: workspace_name
params.label: Workspace name
params.help: Letters, numbers and dashes, 4-63 characters. Names the workspace in the generated draft.

params.name: resource_group
params.label: Resource group
params.help: The resource group this workspace belongs to.

params.name: sku
params.label: Pricing tier
params.help: PerGB2018 bills per GB ingested and is the standard choice for a new workspace. CapacityReservation commits to a fixed daily volume at a lower per-GB rate for high-ingestion workspaces. Older legacy tiers are not offered.

params.name: retention_in_days
params.label: Retention (days)
params.help: Days of log data to retain; Azure accepts 30-730. Longer retention raises storage cost.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Workspace name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this workspace collects
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this workspace.

decisions: If Capacity Reservation is chosen for the pricing tier, also set a daily reservation volume — not captured as a param here.
A daily ingestion cap and diagnostic settings that route other resources' logs into this workspace are configured separately after creation.
